description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 13: The Transport Museum (Distance, Time, Money, and Practical Math)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of money, addition, time, and distance. Can fast-track through core museum math content with challenge problems.

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to distance calculations with kilometres and metres
Learn elapsed time calculations for travel journeys
Practice money math with ticket prices
Apply multiplication to real museum problems
Challenge: Word problems combining addition, subtraction, and transport

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of all transport museum math topics

videoSequence.reason: Practical math at the museum using multiplication
Word problems with addition and subtraction in transport context

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in money, addition, or time concepts. Needs targeted review before tackling transport museum math.

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Indian money - rupees and paise
Review: Telling time in hours and minutes

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in money, addition, and time before attempting transport museum math

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Review Indian money - rupees and paise
Essential: Review adding 3 and 4 digit numbers
Critical: Review telling time in hours and minutes
Begin main content: Money math with ticket prices

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with the Transport Museum chapter.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

time_basics.conceptName: Telling time in hours and minutes

distance_concept.conceptName: Basic understanding of distance and measurement

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: A car travels 150 km from Delhi to Mathura and then 50 km more to Agra. What is the total distance?
Which is farther: 3 kilometres or 300 metres?
questions.explanation: 3 kilometres = 3000 metres, which is much farther than 300 metres.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand distance calculations well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with distance addition, review multi-digit addition

questions.question: A museum ticket costs 25 rupees for a child. How much do 3 tickets cost?
questions.explanation: 3 tickets x 25 rupees = 75 rupees. You can think of it as 25 + 25 + 25 = 75.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for combined word problems

ifWrong.message: Watch the money math video again, then try some practice problems with ticket prices.

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on money basics or addition

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising money counting and addition with real objects at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your money, addition, and time really well. You are ready to explore the Transport Museum. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand money, addition, and time well. Let's visit the Transport Museum with complete coverage of all the topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in money, addition, or time concepts. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to the museum math.

low_score.message: You need to review money basics, addition, and time-telling first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then explore the Transport Museum.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how transport museum math concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

distanceCalculationFluency.description: % of students who can solve basic distance word problems correctly

moneyMathAccuracy.description: % of students who can calculate ticket prices and change correctly

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 50-55 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (25 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 50-60 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasise that kilometres are for long distances (between cities) and metres for shorter distances. Use relatable examples like school to home.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students struggling with elapsed time when minutes cross the hour boundary
commonChallenges.solution2: Use a number line or clock face to count forward. Break into 'minutes to the next hour' then 'remaining hours and minutes'.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students adding money amounts without aligning rupees and paise correctly
commonChallenges.solution3: Practice writing money amounts in a column with rupees and paise separated. Use real coins and notes for hands-on practice.
